---
title: "Shadow Tealbook A"
subtitle: "Current Economic and Financial Conditions"
date: today
date-format: "MMMM D, YYYY"
---

```{python}
#| label: setup
#| include: false

import os, sys, json, re, warnings
import urllib.request
from pathlib import Path
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import plotly.graph_objects as go
import plotly.subplots as sp
warnings.filterwarnings("ignore")

sys.path.insert(0, str(Path('.').resolve().parent))
from data.fred_pulls import pull_series, get_series

# ── Global color & line system ────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Rule: BLUE=primary/actual, RED=secondary/forecast, GRAY=tertiary/alternative
# Solid=primary series, Dash=secondary series, Dot=reference/target
C1   = '#003087'   # Fed blue  — primary series / actuals
C2   = '#C41E3A'   # Fed red   — secondary series / forecasts
C3   = '#6B6B6B'   # Gray      — alternative/reference
C4   = '#2E7D32'   # Green     — special (e.g. PCE growth)
C5   = '#E65100'   # Orange    — volatility indicators
# Aliases kept for backward compat
BLUE = C1; RED = C2; GRAY = C3; GREEN = C4; ORANGE = C5

L_SOLID  = dict(dash='solid')
L_DASH   = dict(dash='dash')
L_DOT    = dict(dash='dot')
W_THICK  = 2.0   # primary line width
W_MED    = 1.5   # secondary line width
W_THIN   = 1.2   # tertiary line width

def line(color, style='solid', width=None):
    w = width if width else (W_THICK if style=='solid' else W_MED)
    return dict(color=color, width=w, dash=style)

# ── Chart windows ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TODAY        = datetime.today()
CHART_START  = (TODAY - timedelta(days=6*365)).strftime('%Y-%m-%d')
CHART_START_GDP = '2015-01-01'
DATA_START   = '2010-01-01'
now_str      = TODAY.strftime('%B %d, %Y')

# ── Date helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
def quarter_label(dt):
    q = (dt.month - 1) // 3 + 1
    return f"Q{q} {dt.year}"

def month_label(dt):
    return dt.strftime('%B %Y')

def latest_label(series, freq='monthly'):
    dt = pd.Timestamp(series.index[-1])
    return quarter_label(dt) if freq == 'quarterly' else month_label(dt)

# ── Auto-counters ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
_tbl_count = 0
_fig_count = 0

def tbl_title(name):
    global _tbl_count
    _tbl_count += 1
    return (f'<p style="color:{C1};font-weight:bold;font-size:0.95rem;'
            f'margin:1.4rem 0 0.3rem 0;">Table {_tbl_count} — {name}</p>')

def next_fig():
    global _fig_count
    _fig_count += 1
    return _fig_count

# ── Plotly layout helpers ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
def fed_layout(title='', height=420, showlegend=True):
    """3-panel subplots layout (Overview/Labor/Inflation/Financial)."""
    return dict(
        title=dict(text=title, font=dict(size=13, color='#222')),
        height=height,
        plot_bgcolor='white', paper_bgcolor='white',
        font=dict(family='Source Sans Pro, Arial, sans-serif', size=11),
        legend=dict(orientation='h', yanchor='bottom', y=1.02,
                    xanchor='left', x=0, font=dict(size=10)),
        showlegend=showlegend,
        margin=dict(l=50, r=30, t=60, b=40),
        xaxis=dict(showgrid=False, zeroline=False, linecolor='#ccc',
                   range=[CHART_START, TODAY.strftime('%Y-%m-%d')]),
        yaxis=dict(showgrid=True, gridcolor='#f0f0f0', zeroline=False),
        hovermode='x unified',
    )

def xaxis_cfg(start=CHART_START_GDP):
    today = pd.Timestamp.now().strftime('%Y-%m-%d')
    return dict(showgrid=True, gridcolor='#eeeeee', zeroline=False,
                tickfont=dict(size=9), dtick='M24', tickformat='%Y',
                type='date', range=[start, today])

def base_layout(title, subtitle=None, start=CHART_START_GDP, r_margin=15):
    """2-per-row GDP/detail chart layout."""
    full_title = (f'<b style="font-size:11px">{title}</b>'
                  + (f'<br><span style="font-size:10px;color:#666">{subtitle}</span>'
                     if subtitle else ''))
    return dict(
        title=dict(text=full_title, x=0.5, xanchor='center', yanchor='top',
                   font=dict(size=11, color=C1), pad=dict(b=8)),
        width=420, height=310,
        margin=dict(l=55, r=r_margin, t=65, b=50),
        paper_bgcolor='white', plot_bgcolor='#fafafa',
        xaxis=xaxis_cfg(start),
        yaxis=dict(showgrid=True, gridcolor='#eeeeee', zeroline=False,
                   tickfont=dict(size=9), autorange=True),
        legend=dict(orientation='h', yanchor='top', y=-0.18,
                    xanchor='center', x=0.5,
                    font=dict(size=9), bgcolor='rgba(0,0,0,0)'),
        font=dict(family='Arial, sans-serif', size=10),
        hovermode='x unified',
    )

# ── GDP figure queue (2-per-row grid) ────────────────────────────────────────
_plotly_js_included = False
_pending = []

def _flush():
    global _pending
    if not _pending:
        return
    if len(_pending) == 1:
        h, s = _pending[0]
        display(HTML(f'<div style="margin-bottom:2rem;">{h}'
                     f'<p style="font-size:0.73rem;color:#777;margin:3px 0 0 0;">{s}</p></div>'))
    else:
        (h1,s1),(h2,s2) = _pending[0], _pending[1]
        display(HTML(f"""
<div style="display:grid;grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr;gap:20px;margin-bottom:2rem;">
  <div>{h1}<p style="font-size:0.73rem;color:#777;margin:3px 0 0 0;">{s1}</p></div>
  <div>{h2}<p style="font-size:0.73rem;color:#777;margin:3px 0 0 0;">{s2}</p></div>
</div>"""))
    _pending = []

def show(fig, source_text, flush=False):
    global _plotly_js_included
    include_js = 'cdn' if not _plotly_js_included else False
    _plotly_js_included = True
    html = fig.to_html(full_html=False, include_plotlyjs=include_js,
                       config={'displayModeBar': False})
    _pending.append((html, source_text))
    if flush or len(_pending) == 2:
        _flush()

# ── LLM helper ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TEALBOOK_SYSTEM = """You are a Federal Reserve staff economist writing a section
of the Tealbook A — the internal briefing document prepared for FOMC meetings.
Style rules:
- Third person, no first-person pronouns
- Every claim must reference a specific number AND its time period
- Present tense for current conditions; disciplined forward-looking language for projections
- No hedging phrases like 'it seems' or 'may suggest'
- Do not invent numbers; only use what is given
- Complete every sentence fully — never truncate mid-sentence
Respond with narrative text only — no headers, no bullet points, no markdown."""

def call_claude(user_prompt, max_tokens=450):
    api_key = os.environ.get('ANTHROPIC_API_KEY', '')
    if not api_key:
        return "_[LLM narrative unavailable — set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY to enable.]_"
    payload = json.dumps({
        "model": "claude-sonnet-4-20250514",
        "max_tokens": max_tokens,
        "system": TEALBOOK_SYSTEM,
        "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": user_prompt}]
    }).encode()
    req = urllib.request.Request(
        "https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages",
        data=payload,
        headers={"Content-Type": "application/json",
                 "x-api-key": api_key,
                 "anthropic-version": "2023-06-01"}
    )
    try:
        with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=30) as resp:
            return json.loads(resp.read())["content"][0]["text"].strip()
    except Exception as e:
        return f"_[LLM call failed: {e}]_"
```

::: {.panel-tabset}

## Overview

```{python}
#| label: overview-data
#| include: false
import io, requests as _req

gdp_g    = pull_series('A191RL1Q225SBEA', start=DATA_START).dropna()
unrate   = pull_series('UNRATE',          start=DATA_START).dropna()
core_pce = pull_series('PCEPILFE',        start=DATA_START).dropna().pct_change(12).mul(100).dropna()
cpi_ov   = pull_series('CPIAUCSL',        start=DATA_START).dropna().pct_change(12).mul(100).dropna()
pce_ov   = pull_series('PCEPI',           start=DATA_START).dropna().pct_change(12).mul(100).dropna()
payrolls = pull_series('PAYEMS',          start=DATA_START).dropna().diff().dropna()
nrou     = pull_series('NROU',            start=DATA_START).dropna().resample('MS').interpolate()
t10      = pull_series('DGS10',           start=DATA_START).dropna().resample('MS').mean()
t2       = pull_series('DGS2',            start=DATA_START).dropna().resample('MS').mean()

gdp_latest    = gdp_g.iloc[-1]
gdp_prev      = gdp_g.iloc[-2]
gdp_date      = quarter_label(pd.Timestamp(gdp_g.index[-1]))
gdp_prev_date = quarter_label(pd.Timestamp(gdp_g.index[-2]))
unrate_latest = unrate.iloc[-1]
unrate_date   = month_label(pd.Timestamp(unrate.index[-1]))
nrou_latest   = nrou.reindex(unrate.index, method='ffill').iloc[-1]
ugap          = unrate_latest - nrou_latest
core_latest   = core_pce.iloc[-1]
core_date     = month_label(pd.Timestamp(core_pce.index[-1]))
pce_latest    = pce_ov.iloc[-1]
pce_date_ov   = month_label(pd.Timestamp(pce_ov.index[-1]))
cpi_latest    = cpi_ov.iloc[-1]
cpi_date_ov   = month_label(pd.Timestamp(cpi_ov.index[-1]))
payroll_3m    = payrolls.iloc[-3:].mean()
payroll_date  = month_label(pd.Timestamp(payrolls.index[-1]))
t10_latest    = t10.iloc[-1]
t10_date      = month_label(pd.Timestamp(t10.index[-1]))
spread_10_2   = (t10 - t2).dropna().iloc[-1]

# SPF / FOMC SEP — update each cycle
spf_gdp_next  = 2.1
spf_pce_next  = 2.3
fomc_lr_gdp   = 1.8
fomc_lr_unemp = 4.1
fomc_lr_pce   = 2.0

# ── Pull SPF for comparison table ────────────────────────────────────────────
SPF_BASE_OV = ('https://www.philadelphiafed.org/-/media/FRBP/Assets/'
               'Surveys-And-Data/survey-of-professional-forecasters/'
               'data-files/files/Median_{}_Growth.xlsx')

def _spf_annual(name, row_idx=-1):
    """Pull SPF Q4/Q4 annual forecasts for comparison table."""
    try:
        r = _req.get(SPF_BASE_OV.format(name), timeout=20)
        r.raise_for_status()
        df = pd.read_excel(io.BytesIO(r.content), engine='openpyxl')
        df.columns = [str(c).strip() for c in df.columns]
        df_c = df.dropna(subset=['YEAR','QUARTER'])
        prefix = f'D{name}'
        col2 = f'{prefix}2'
        if col2 in df_c.columns:
            df_c = df_c[df_c[col2].notna()]
        if len(df_c) < abs(row_idx):
            return {}
        row  = df_c.iloc[row_idx]
        year = int(row['YEAR'])
        qtr  = int(row['QUARTER'])
        out = {}
        for h in range(2, 7):
            col = f'{prefix}{h}'
            if col in row.index and pd.notna(row[col]):
                offset = h - 2
                total  = (qtr - 1) + offset
                y_out  = year + total // 4
                q_out  = (total % 4) + 1
                out[f'Q{q_out} {y_out}'] = round(float(row[col]), 1)
        return out
    except:
        return {}

# Current and prior SPF vintages for GDP, PCE, Unemployment
spf_gdp_curr  = _spf_annual('RGDP',   row_idx=-1)
spf_gdp_prior = _spf_annual('RGDP',   row_idx=-2)
spf_pce_curr  = _spf_annual('RPCE',   row_idx=-1)   # real PCE proxy
spf_pce_prior = _spf_annual('RPCE',   row_idx=-2)
spf_unemp_curr  = _spf_annual('UNEMP', row_idx=-1)
spf_unemp_prior = _spf_annual('UNEMP', row_idx=-2)

# Get the forecast years (next 2 full years from latest SPF)
_spf_qs = list(spf_gdp_curr.keys())
_yr1 = int(_spf_qs[0].split()[1]) if _spf_qs else TODAY.year
_yr2 = _yr1 + 1
```

```{python}
#| label: overview-comparison-table
from IPython.display import display, HTML

def _fv(d, yr, default='n.a.'):
    """Get Q4 value for a given year from SPF dict."""
    key = f'Q4 {yr}'
    v = d.get(key)
    return f'{v:.1f}' if v is not None else default

rows_data = [
    ('GDP (Q4/Q4 percent change)', [
        ('Current SPF',  _fv(spf_gdp_curr, _yr1),  _fv(spf_gdp_curr, _yr2)),
        ('Prior SPF',    _fv(spf_gdp_prior, _yr1),  _fv(spf_gdp_prior, _yr2)),
        ('FOMC LR',      f'{fomc_lr_gdp:.1f}',      f'{fomc_lr_gdp:.1f}'),
    ]),
    ('Unemployment rate (Q4 level)', [
        ('Current SPF',  _fv(spf_unemp_curr, _yr1),  _fv(spf_unemp_curr, _yr2)),
        ('Prior SPF',    _fv(spf_unemp_prior, _yr1),  _fv(spf_unemp_prior, _yr2)),
        ('FOMC LR',      f'{fomc_lr_unemp:.1f}',       f'{fomc_lr_unemp:.1f}'),
    ]),
    ('PCE price inflation (Q4/Q4 percent change)', [
        ('Current SPF',  _fv(spf_pce_curr, _yr1),  _fv(spf_pce_curr, _yr2)),
        ('Prior SPF',    _fv(spf_pce_prior, _yr1),  _fv(spf_pce_prior, _yr2)),
        ('FOMC target',  f'{fomc_lr_pce:.1f}',      f'{fomc_lr_pce:.1f}'),
    ]),
]

tbl_html = f"""
{tbl_title('Comparison of Current and Prior SPF Forecasts')}
<table style="width:min(70%,600px);border-collapse:collapse;
              font-family:'Source Sans Pro',Arial,sans-serif;font-size:0.88rem;">
  <thead>
    <tr style="background:{C1};color:white;">
      <th style="padding:7px 12px;text-align:left;"></th>
      <th style="padding:7px 12px;text-align:right;">{_yr1}</th>
      <th style="padding:7px 12px;text-align:right;">{_yr2}</th>
    </tr>
  </thead><tbody>"""

for group_label, sub_rows in rows_data:
    tbl_html += f"""<tr><td colspan="3" style="padding:8px 12px 3px 12px;
        font-weight:bold;font-size:0.88rem;border-top:1px solid #ccc;">
        {group_label}</td></tr>"""
    for i,(lbl, v1, v2) in enumerate(sub_rows):
        bg = '#f5f7fb' if i==0 else ('white' if i==1 else '#f8fff8')
        is_curr = (i==0)
        fw = 'font-weight:600;' if is_curr else ''
        tbl_html += f"""<tr style="background:{bg};">
          <td style="padding:4px 12px 4px 24px;{fw}">{lbl}</td>
          <td style="padding:4px 12px;text-align:right;{fw}">{v1}</td>
          <td style="padding:4px 12px;text-align:right;{fw}">{v2}</td>
        </tr>"""

tbl_html += f"""</tbody>
  <tfoot><tr style="background:#f0f0f0;font-size:0.76rem;color:#777;">
    <td colspan="3" style="padding:5px 12px;">
      Source: Philadelphia Fed Survey of Professional Forecasters.
      FOMC longer-run values from SEP. n.a. = not available.
    </td>
  </tr></tfoot>
</table>"""
display(HTML(tbl_html))
```

```{python}
#| label: overview-narrative
#| output: asis

prompt = f"""Current economic conditions as of {now_str}:
- Real GDP growth ({gdp_date}, SAAR): {gdp_latest:.1f}%; prior quarter ({gdp_prev_date}): {gdp_prev:.1f}%
- Unemployment rate ({unrate_date}): {unrate_latest:.1f}%; natural rate: {nrou_latest:.1f}%; gap: {ugap:+.1f} pp
- Core PCE inflation YoY ({core_date}): {core_latest:.1f}%; Fed target: {fomc_lr_pce:.1f}%
- Average monthly payroll gains, 3-month average through {payroll_date}: {payroll_3m:,.0f}K
- 10-year Treasury yield ({t10_date}): {t10_latest:.2f}%; 10s-2s spread: {spread_10_2:+.2f} pp
- SPF projections: GDP {spf_gdp_next}%, PCE {spf_pce_next}% (next year medians)
- FOMC longer-run SEP: GDP {fomc_lr_gdp}%, unemployment {fomc_lr_unemp}%, PCE {fomc_lr_pce}%

Write a 2-paragraph overview. Paragraph 1: real activity and labor market.
Paragraph 2: inflation and financial conditions.
Every sentence must include the specific time period it references."""

print(call_claude(prompt))
```

```{python}
#| label: plot-overview-1

from IPython.display import display, HTML

fn = next_fig()
fig1 = go.Figure()
g = gdp_g[CHART_START:]
fig1.add_trace(go.Bar(x=g.index, y=g.values,
    marker_color=[C1 if v>=0 else C2 for v in g], name='GDP growth',
    hovertemplate='%{x|Q%q %Y}: %{y:.1f}%<extra></extra>'))
fig1.add_hline(y=0, line_width=0.7, line_color='black')
fig1.update_layout(**fed_layout(f'Figure {fn} — Real GDP Growth (%, SAAR)', height=320))
fig1.update_xaxes(range=[CHART_START, TODAY.strftime('%Y-%m-%d')])

fn2 = next_fig()
fig2 = go.Figure()
ur = unrate[CHART_START:]
nr = nrou.reindex(ur.index, method='ffill')
fig2.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=ur.index, y=ur.values, name='Unemployment',
    line=line(C1), hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:.1f}%<extra></extra>'))
fig2.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=nr.index, y=nr.values, name='Natural rate (CBO)',
    line=line(C2,'dash'), hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:.1f}%<extra></extra>'))
fig2.add_hline(y=fomc_lr_unemp, line_color=C3, line_dash='dot', line_width=1,
    annotation_text=f'LR {fomc_lr_unemp}%', annotation_position='top right')
fig2.update_layout(**fed_layout(f'Figure {fn2} — Unemployment Rate (%)', height=320))
fig2.update_xaxes(range=[CHART_START, TODAY.strftime('%Y-%m-%d')])

h1 = fig1.to_html(full_html=False, include_plotlyjs='cdn', config={'displayModeBar':False})
h2 = fig2.to_html(full_html=False, include_plotlyjs=False, config={'displayModeBar':False})
display(HTML(f"""
<div style="display:grid;grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr;gap:20px;margin-bottom:1.5rem;">
  <div>{h1}</div><div>{h2}</div>
</div>"""))
```

```{python}
#| label: plot-overview-2

fn3 = next_fig()
fig3 = go.Figure()
cp = core_pce[CHART_START:]
fig3.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=cp.index, y=cp.values, name='Core PCE',
    line=line(C1), hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:.2f}%<extra></extra>'))
fig3.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=pce_ov[CHART_START:].index, y=pce_ov[CHART_START:].values,
    name='Headline PCE', line=line(C2,'dash'),
    hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:.2f}%<extra></extra>'))
fig3.add_hline(y=2.0, line_color=C3, line_dash='dot', line_width=1,
    annotation_text='2% target', annotation_position='top right')
fig3.update_layout(**fed_layout(f'Figure {fn3} — PCE Inflation (YoY %)', height=320))
fig3.update_xaxes(range=[CHART_START, TODAY.strftime('%Y-%m-%d')])

fn4 = next_fig()
fig4 = go.Figure()
ur_hist = unrate[CHART_START:]
fig4.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=ur_hist.index, y=ur_hist.values, name='Unemployment',
    line=line(C1), hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:.1f}%<extra></extra>'))

# Add SPF forecast dots for Q4 values
for yr, d_curr, d_prior in [(_yr1, spf_unemp_curr, spf_unemp_prior),
                              (_yr2, spf_unemp_curr, spf_unemp_prior)]:
    key = f'Q4 {yr}'
    vc = d_curr.get(key)
    vp = d_prior.get(key)
    if vc:
        fig4.add_trace(go.Scatter(
            x=[pd.Timestamp(f'{yr}-12-01')], y=[vc], mode='markers',
            name=f'SPF {yr}', marker=dict(color=C2, size=8, symbol='circle'),
            hovertemplate=f'SPF Q4/{yr}: {vc:.1f}%<extra></extra>',
            showlegend=(yr==_yr1)))
    if vp:
        fig4.add_trace(go.Scatter(
            x=[pd.Timestamp(f'{yr}-12-01')], y=[vp], mode='markers',
            name=f'Prior SPF {yr}', marker=dict(color=C3, size=8, symbol='circle-open'),
            hovertemplate=f'Prior SPF Q4/{yr}: {vp:.1f}%<extra></extra>',
            showlegend=(yr==_yr1)))

fig4.add_hline(y=fomc_lr_unemp, line_color=C3, line_dash='dot', line_width=1,
    annotation_text=f'LR {fomc_lr_unemp}%', annotation_position='bottom right')
fig4.update_layout(**fed_layout(f'Figure {fn4} — Unemployment: History & SPF Forecast (%)', height=320))
fig4.update_xaxes(range=[CHART_START, f'{_yr2+1}-01-01'])

h3 = fig3.to_html(full_html=False, include_plotlyjs=False, config={'displayModeBar':False})
h4 = fig4.to_html(full_html=False, include_plotlyjs=False, config={'displayModeBar':False})
display(HTML(f"""
<div style="display:grid;grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr;gap:20px;margin-bottom:1.5rem;">
  <div>{h3}</div><div>{h4}</div>
</div>"""))
```

---
## Real GDP

```{python}
#| label: gdp-setup
#| include: false

import io
try:
    import requests
except ImportError:
    import subprocess, sys
    subprocess.check_call([sys.executable,'-m','pip','install','requests'])
    import requests

SPF_BASE = ('https://www.philadelphiafed.org/-/media/FRBP/Assets/'
            'Surveys-And-Data/survey-of-professional-forecasters/'
            'data-files/files/Median_{}_Growth.xlsx')

def pull_spf(spf_name, row_index=-1):
    try:
        r = requests.get(SPF_BASE.format(spf_name), timeout=20)
        r.raise_for_status()
        df = pd.read_excel(io.BytesIO(r.content), engine='openpyxl')
        df.columns = [str(c).strip() for c in df.columns]
        prefix = f'D{spf_name}'
        col_now = f'{prefix}2'
        df_clean = df.dropna(subset=['YEAR', 'QUARTER'])
        if col_now in df_clean.columns:
            df_clean = df_clean[df_clean[col_now].notna()]
        if df_clean.empty or len(df_clean) < abs(row_index):
            return {}
        row  = df_clean.iloc[row_index]
        year = int(row['YEAR'])
        qtr  = int(row['QUARTER'])
        result = {}
        for h in range(2, 7):
            col = f'{prefix}{h}'
            if col in row.index and pd.notna(row[col]):
                offset = h - 2
                total  = (qtr - 1) + offset
                q_year = year + total // 4
                q_num  = (total % 4) + 1
                result[f'Q{q_num} {q_year}'] = round(float(row[col]), 1)
        return result
    except Exception as e:
        print(f'  [WARNING] SPF {spf_name}: {e}')
        return {}

COMPONENTS = [
    ('Real GDP',                'A191RL1Q225SBEA', 'A191RL1Q225SBEA', 'RGDP'),
    ('PCE',                     'DPCERL1Q225SBEA', 'DPCERY2Q224SBEA', 'RCONSUM'),
    ('Nonres. fixed investment','A008RL1Q225SBEA', 'A008RY2Q224SBEA', 'RNRESIN'),
    ('Residential investment',  'A011RL1Q225SBEA', 'A011RY2Q224SBEA', 'RRESINV'),
    ('Federal government',      'A823RL1Q225SBEA', 'A823RY2Q224SBEA', 'RFEDGOV'),
    ('State & local govt',      'A829RL1Q225SBEA', 'A829RY2Q224SBEA', 'RSLGOV'),
    ('Change in inventories',   None,              'A014RY2Q224SBEA', 'RCBI'),
    ('Net exports',             None,              'A019RY2Q224SBEA', 'REXPORT'),
]

def last2(ticker):
    if ticker is None:
        return None, None, None, None
    s = pull_series(ticker, start=DATA_START).dropna()
    if len(s) < 2:
        return None, None, None, None
    return (quarter_label(pd.Timestamp(s.index[-2])), round(float(s.iloc[-2]), 2),
            quarter_label(pd.Timestamp(s.index[-1])), round(float(s.iloc[-1]), 2))

pot   = pull_series('GDPPOT', start=DATA_START).dropna()
pot_g = (((pot / pot.shift(1)) ** 4) - 1).mul(100).dropna()

def pot_for_quarter(ql):
    for dt, val in pot_g.items():
        if quarter_label(pd.Timestamp(dt)) == ql:
            return round(float(val), 1)
    return None

gdp_s       = pull_series('A191RL1Q225SBEA', start=DATA_START).dropna()
gdp_vintage = f"BEA release through {quarter_label(pd.Timestamp(gdp_s.index[-1]))}"

saar_rows    = []
contrib_rows = []
spf_all      = {}
spf_prev_all = {}

for label, saar_t, contrib_t, spf_name in COMPONENTS:
    qp, vp, qc, vc         = last2(saar_t)
    qp_c, vp_c, qc_c, vc_c = last2(contrib_t)
    spf_d      = pull_spf(spf_name, row_index=-1)
    spf_d_prev = pull_spf(spf_name, row_index=-2)
    spf_all[label]      = spf_d
    spf_prev_all[label] = spf_d_prev
    bold = (label == 'Real GDP')
    saar_rows.append({'label': label, 'bold': bold,
                      'q_prev': qp, 'v_prev': vp,
                      'q_curr': qc, 'v_curr': vc,
                      'spf': spf_d, 'spf_prev': spf_d_prev,
                      'no_saar': saar_t is None})
    contrib_rows.append({'label': label, 'bold': bold,
                         'q_prev': qp_c, 'v_prev': vp_c,
                         'q_curr': qc_c, 'v_curr': vc_c})

gdp_row      = saar_rows[0]
q_prev       = gdp_row['q_prev']
q_curr       = gdp_row['q_curr']
spf_gdp      = spf_all['Real GDP']
spf_gdp_prev = spf_prev_all['Real GDP']
spf_qs       = list(spf_gdp.keys())
gdp_curr_v   = gdp_row['v_curr']
gdp_prev_v   = gdp_row['v_prev']

def _sv(label, q):  return spf_all.get(label, {}).get(q)
def _spv(label, q): return spf_prev_all.get(label, {}).get(q)
def _cv(label):
    r = next((x for x in contrib_rows if x['label']==label), {})
    return r.get('v_curr')
def _av(label):
    r = next((x for x in saar_rows if x['label']==label), {})
    return r.get('v_curr')
def _f(v):  return f'{v:.1f}' if v is not None else 'n/a'
def _fp(v): return f'{v:+.1f}' if v is not None else 'n/a'

spf_q1 = spf_qs[0] if spf_qs else 'Q+1'
spf_q2 = spf_qs[1] if len(spf_qs)>1 else 'Q+2'

gdp_lvl   = pull_series('GDPC1',  start=DATA_START).dropna()
pot_lvl   = pull_series('GDPPOT', start=DATA_START).dropna()
pot_align = pot_lvl.reindex(gdp_lvl.index, method='ffill')
og_series = ((gdp_lvl - pot_align) / pot_align * 100).dropna()
og_latest = round(float(og_series.iloc[-1]), 2)
og_date   = quarter_label(pd.Timestamp(og_series.index[-1]))
```

```{python}
#| label: gdp-beigebook
#| include: false

BEIGE_BOOK_CACHE = Path('../data/beige_book_latest.txt')

def fetch_beige_book():
    try:
        from dateutil.relativedelta import relativedelta
        import urllib3
        headers = {'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36'}
        now = datetime.today()
        for m in range(12):
            dt     = now - relativedelta(months=m)
            yyyymm = dt.strftime('%Y%m')
            url    = f"https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/beigebook{yyyymm}-summary.htm"
            for verify in [True, False]:
                try:
                    r = requests.get(url, headers=headers, timeout=15, verify=verify)
                    if r.status_code == 404: break
                    if r.status_code != 200: continue
                    text  = re.sub(r'<[^>]+>', ' ', r.text)
                    text  = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', text).strip()
                    start = re.search(r'Economic activity', text, re.IGNORECASE)
                    end   = re.search(r'Federal Reserve Bank of Boston', text, re.IGNORECASE)
                    if start and end and end.start() > start.start():
                        summary = text[start.start():end.start()].strip()
                    elif start:
                        summary = text[start.start():start.start()+2000].strip()
                    else: break
                    if len(summary) < 100: break
                    if len(summary) > 2000: summary = summary[:2000] + '...'
                    date_match = re.search(
                        r'(January|February|March|April|May|June|July|August|'
                        r'September|October|November|December)\s+\d{1,2},?\s+\d{4}', text)
                    bb_date = date_match.group(0) if date_match else dt.strftime('%B %Y')
                    BEIGE_BOOK_CACHE.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
                    BEIGE_BOOK_CACHE.write_text(f'{bb_date}\n{summary}', encoding='utf-8')
                    print(f'[Beige Book] Fetched: {bb_date}')
                    return summary, bb_date
                except requests.exceptions.SSLError:
                    if verify:
                        urllib3.disable_warnings()
                        continue
                    break
                except Exception as e:
                    print(f'[Beige Book] {yyyymm} verify={verify}: {e}')
                    break
        if BEIGE_BOOK_CACHE.exists():
            lines = BEIGE_BOOK_CACHE.read_text(encoding='utf-8').split('\n', 1)
            if len(lines) == 2 and len(lines[1]) > 100:
                print(f'[Beige Book] Loaded from cache: {lines[0]}')
                return lines[1].strip(), lines[0].strip()
        print('[WARNING] Beige Book unavailable')
        return None, None
    except Exception as e:
        print(f'[WARNING] Beige Book fetch failed: {e}')
        return None, None

beige_text, beige_date = fetch_beige_book()
```

```{python}
#| label: gdp-narrative-1-recent
#| output: asis

pdfp = (_cv('PCE') or 0) + (_cv('Nonres. fixed investment') or 0) + (_cv('Residential investment') or 0)

# Beige Book is current-quarter context — label explicitly so model doesn't confuse periods
bb_fwd = (f"\n\nBEIGE BOOK CONTEXT — reflects {spf_q1} conditions, NOT {q_curr} GDP release "
          f"({beige_date}):\n{beige_text[:600]}") if beige_text else ""

prompt_1 = f"""You are a Federal Reserve staff economist writing the GDP & Output section of Tealbook A.
Style: declarative, third person, precise. No hedging. Every sentence names its exact time period
and a specific numerical value. Flowing prose — no bullet points, no headers.
Complete every sentence fully. Do not invent facts beyond what is provided.

IMPORTANT TEMPORAL STRUCTURE:
- {q_curr} = most recently RELEASED GDP quarter (backward-looking, realized data)
- {spf_q1} = CURRENT quarter underway (not yet released; Beige Book period)
The Beige Book reflects {spf_q1} conditions, NOT {q_curr}.

REALIZED DATA ({q_curr}):
Real GDP: {_f(gdp_curr_v)}% SAAR | prior quarter {q_prev}: {_f(gdp_prev_v)}% SAAR
Private domestic final purchases: {pdfp:+.1f} pp (PCE + nonres. + residential contributions)
{bb_fwd}

TASK — Write exactly 1 paragraph (3 sentences):
1. State the headline GDP growth rate in {q_curr} and whether it accelerated or
   decelerated relative to {q_prev}, noting the exact magnitude of the change.
2. State whether private domestic final purchases confirm or diverge from the
   headline, using it as a signal of underlying momentum in {q_curr}.
3. If Beige Book is provided, note ONE observation about {spf_q1} conditions
   that either supports continuity with or signals a shift from the {q_curr} trend.
   Explicitly reference {spf_q1} as the period and use qualified language."""

print(call_claude(prompt_1, max_tokens=280))
```

```{python}
#| label: gdp-narrative-2-components
#| output: asis

prompt_2 = f"""You are a Federal Reserve staff economist writing the GDP & Output section of Tealbook A.
Style: declarative, third person. Every sentence names its exact time period and a specific value.
Two bullet paragraphs beginning with •. Complete every sentence fully — never truncate.

COMPONENT CONTRIBUTIONS TO {q_curr} GDP GROWTH (realized, percentage points):
  PCE:                   {_fp(_cv('PCE'))} pp  |  PCE SAAR: {_f(_av('PCE'))}%
  Nonres. fixed inv.:    {_fp(_cv('Nonres. fixed investment'))} pp  |  SAAR: {_f(_av('Nonres. fixed investment'))}%
  Residential inv.:      {_fp(_cv('Residential investment'))} pp  |  SAAR: {_f(_av('Residential investment'))}%
  Federal government:    {_fp(_cv('Federal government'))} pp  |  SAAR: {_f(_av('Federal government'))}%
  State & local govt:    {_fp(_cv('State & local govt'))} pp  |  SAAR: {_f(_av('State & local govt'))}%
  Change in inventories: {_fp(_cv('Change in inventories'))} pp
  Net exports:           {_fp(_cv('Net exports'))} pp
All data pertain to {q_curr}.

• Bullet 1 (Spending components): Name the two largest positive contributors to {q_curr}
  GDP growth with pp values. Name the largest drag. Note whether consumer spending
  and business investment moved in the same direction in {q_curr}.

• Bullet 2 (Inventories and trade): State the inventory and net export contributions
  in {q_curr}. Note whether their combined swing distorts the headline relative to
  underlying final demand. Flag either component if unusually large (beyond ±0.5 pp)
  and interpret as likely transitory or structural."""

print(call_claude(prompt_2, max_tokens=320))
```

```{python}
#| label: gdp-narrative-3-pce-housing
#| output: asis

prompt_3 = f"""You are a Federal Reserve staff economist writing the GDP & Output section of Tealbook A.
Style: declarative, third person. Every sentence names its exact time period and value.
Two bullet paragraphs beginning with •. Complete every sentence fully.
Forward-looking language: 'is projected to', 'is expected to', 'appears on track to'.

IMPORTANT: {q_curr} = realized. {spf_q1}, {spf_q2} = projections not yet released.

PCE ({q_curr}): contribution {_fp(_cv('PCE'))} pp | SAAR: {_f(_av('PCE'))}%
PCE SPF current:  {', '.join(f'{q}: {_f(_sv("PCE",q))}%' for q in spf_qs)}
PCE SPF prior:    {', '.join(f'{q}: {_f(_spv("PCE",q))}%' for q in spf_qs if _spv("PCE",q) is not None)}

Residential inv. ({q_curr}): {_f(_av('Residential investment'))}% SAAR | contribution: {_fp(_cv('Residential investment'))} pp
Residential SPF:  {', '.join(f'{q}: {_f(_sv("Residential investment",q))}%' for q in spf_qs)}

• Consumer spending: State PCE growth in {q_curr} and its pp contribution. Compare to
  the prior quarter's pace, noting acceleration or deceleration. Provide one economic
  mechanism linking labor market or financial conditions to the {q_curr} outcome.
  State the SPF projection for {spf_q1} and whether it represents a revision up or
  down from the prior vintage, citing both values.

• Residential investment: State the growth rate and pp contribution in {q_curr}.
  Characterize housing activity as recovering, contracting, or stable relative to
  recent quarters. Name the primary driver (mortgage rates, affordability, or demand).
  State the SPF near-term trajectory for {spf_q1}."""

print(call_claude(prompt_3, max_tokens=340))
```

```{python}
#| label: gdp-narrative-4-bfi-trade
#| output: asis

prompt_4 = f"""You are a Federal Reserve staff economist writing the GDP & Output section of Tealbook A.
Style: declarative, third person. Every sentence names its exact time period and value.
Three bullet paragraphs beginning with •. Complete every sentence fully.
IMPORTANT: {q_curr} = realized. {spf_q1} = current quarter projection.

Nonres. fixed inv. ({q_curr}): {_f(_av('Nonres. fixed investment'))}% SAAR | contribution: {_fp(_cv('Nonres. fixed investment'))} pp
BFI SPF: {', '.join(f'{q}: {_f(_sv("Nonres. fixed investment",q))}%' for q in spf_qs)}
Federal govt ({q_curr}): {_f(_av('Federal government'))}% SAAR | contribution: {_fp(_cv('Federal government'))} pp
State & local ({q_curr}): {_f(_av('State & local govt'))}% SAAR | contribution: {_fp(_cv('State & local govt'))} pp
Inventories ({q_curr}): {_fp(_cv('Change in inventories'))} pp
Net exports ({q_curr}): {_fp(_cv('Net exports'))} pp

• Business fixed investment: State growth and contribution in {q_curr}. Note
  acceleration or deceleration from the prior quarter. Provide one economic mechanism
  (financing conditions, profits, policy uncertainty). State the SPF projection
  for {spf_q1} and whether investment is expected to strengthen or moderate.

• Government spending: State the combined federal and state/local contribution in
  {q_curr}. Characterize the fiscal impulse as expansionary, contractionary, or
  neutral. Note whether the federal component is likely to reverse or persist
  into {spf_q1}, given the direction of the {q_curr} reading.

• Net exports and inventories: State both contributions in {q_curr} with their
  pp values. Note whether they jointly boosted or dragged headline growth. If
  inventories are large in magnitude, interpret as likely transitory. Conclude
  with whether underlying final demand appears stronger or weaker than headline GDP
  after removing these volatile components."""

print(call_claude(prompt_4, max_tokens=420))
```

```{python}
#| label: gdp-growth-table
#| output: asis
from IPython.display import display, HTML

def signed(v, bold=False):
    if v is None or (isinstance(v, float) and np.isnan(v)):
        return '<span style="color:#ccc">—</span>'
    color = C1 if v > 0 else (C2 if v < 0 else '#333')
    s = f'{v:+.1f}'
    return (f'<strong style="color:{color}">{s}</strong>'
            if bold else f'<span style="color:{color}">{s}</span>')

def plain(v, bold=False):
    if v is None or (isinstance(v, float) and np.isnan(v)):
        return '<span style="color:#ccc">—</span>'
    s = f'{v:.1f}'
    return f'<strong>{s}</strong>' if bold else s

all_q_labels = [q_prev, q_curr] + spf_qs
hdrs    = ['Measure'] + all_q_labels
act_set = {q_prev, q_curr}

header_cells = ''.join(
    f'<th style="padding:7px 10px;text-align:{"left" if i==0 else "center"};'
    f'white-space:nowrap;'
    f'{"background:#003087;" if ql in act_set or i==0 else "background:#1a5276;"}'
    f'color:white;font-size:0.82rem;">'
    f'{"Measure" if i==0 else (ql if ql in act_set else ql + "f")}</th>'
    for i, ql in enumerate(hdrs)
)

rows_html = ''
for r in saar_rows:
    if r['no_saar']:
        continue
    b   = r['bold']
    bg  = '#eef2fb' if b else 'white'
    ind = '' if b else '&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;'
    lbl = f'<strong>{r["label"]}</strong>' if b else f'{ind}{r["label"]}'

    cells = (f'<td style="padding:5px 10px;text-align:left;'
             f'border-bottom:none;font-size:0.87rem;">{lbl}</td>')
    for ql in all_q_labels:
        if ql == q_prev:   v = r['v_prev']
        elif ql == q_curr: v = r['v_curr']
        else:              v = r['spf'].get(ql)
        is_fcast = ql not in act_set
        fmt_v    = plain(v, b)
        c_style  = 'color:#1a5276;' if is_fcast and v is not None else ''
        cells += (f'<td style="padding:5px 10px;text-align:center;'
                  f'border-bottom:none;font-size:0.87rem;{c_style}">{fmt_v}</td>')
    rows_html += f'<tr style="background:{bg};">{cells}</tr>'

    if not b:
        sub = (f'<td style="padding:1px 10px 5px 20px;text-align:left;'
               f'border-bottom:1px solid #eee;font-size:0.78rem;color:#888;'
               f'font-style:italic;">Prior forecast</td>')
        for ql in all_q_labels:
            if ql not in act_set:
                pv = r['spf_prev'].get(ql)
                sub += (f'<td style="padding:1px 10px 5px;text-align:center;'
                        f'border-bottom:1px solid #eee;font-size:0.78rem;'
                        f'color:#888;font-style:italic;">{plain(pv)}</td>')
            else:
                sub += (f'<td style="padding:1px 10px 5px;text-align:center;'
                        f'border-bottom:1px solid #eee;font-size:0.78rem;color:#ccc;">—</td>')
        rows_html += f'<tr style="background:{bg};">{sub}</tr>'

# Contributions separator
rows_html += f"""<tr style="background:#f0f4ff;">
  <td colspan="{len(hdrs)}" style="padding:6px 10px;font-size:0.78rem;
     font-weight:bold;color:#555;border-top:2px solid #ccc;border-bottom:1px solid #eee;">
     Contributions (pp) — inventories &amp; net exports (no SPF available)
  </td></tr>"""

for r in saar_rows:
    if not r['no_saar']:
        continue
    cr  = next((x for x in contrib_rows if x['label'] == r['label']), {})
    lbl = f'&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;{r["label"]}'
    cells = (f'<td style="padding:5px 10px;text-align:left;'
             f'border-bottom:1px solid #eee;font-size:0.87rem;">{lbl}</td>')
    for ql in all_q_labels:
        if ql == q_prev:   v = cr.get('v_prev')
        elif ql == q_curr: v = cr.get('v_curr')
        else:              v = None
        fmt_v = signed(v) if v is not None else '<span style="color:#ccc">—</span>'
        cells += (f'<td style="padding:5px 10px;text-align:center;'
                  f'border-bottom:1px solid #eee;font-size:0.87rem;">{fmt_v}</td>')
    rows_html += f'<tr style="background:white;">{cells}</tr>'

pot_cells = ('<td style="padding:6px 10px;text-align:left;border-bottom:1px solid #eee;'
             'font-size:0.87rem;color:#555;font-style:italic;">'
             '&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Potential GDP growth (CBO)</td>')
for ql in all_q_labels:
    pv = pot_for_quarter(ql)
    pot_cells += (f'<td style="padding:6px 10px;text-align:center;'
                  f'border-bottom:1px solid #eee;font-size:0.87rem;'
                  f'color:#555;font-style:italic;">{plain(pv)}</td>')
rows_html += f'<tr style="background:#f8f9ff;">{pot_cells}</tr>'

display(HTML(f"""
{tbl_title('Real GDP Components — Percent Change at Annual Rate (SAAR)')}
<table style="width:min(95%, 960px);border-collapse:collapse;
              font-family:'Source Sans Pro',Arial,sans-serif;">
  <thead><tr>{header_cells}</tr></thead>
  <tbody>{rows_html}</tbody>
</table>
<p style="font-size:0.75rem;color:#888;margin:4px 0 1.2rem 0;">
  Source: Actuals from BEA ({gdp_vintage}); Forecasts from Philadelphia Fed SPF median (columns marked <em>f</em>).<br>
  <em>Prior forecast</em> sub-rows show previous quarter's SPF vintage for the same horizon.
  Inventories and net exports shown as contributions (pp); no SPF published for these components.
  Italicized row: CBO potential GDP growth rate (GDPPOT).
</p>"""))
```

```{python}
#| label: gdp-narrative-5-nearterm
#| output: asis

spf_lines = '\n'.join(
    f'  {q}: GDP {_f(spf_gdp.get(q))}% | PCE {_f(_sv("PCE",q))}% | '
    f'Nonres. {_f(_sv("Nonres. fixed investment",q))}% | Res. {_f(_sv("Residential investment",q))}%'
    for q in spf_qs
) or '  SPF data unavailable'

spf_prev_lines = '\n'.join(
    f'  {q}: GDP {_f(spf_gdp_prev.get(q))}%'
    for q in spf_qs if spf_gdp_prev.get(q) is not None
) or '  Prior SPF unavailable'

bb_nearterm = (f"\nBEIGE BOOK — {spf_q1} current conditions ({beige_date}):\n{beige_text[:800]}") if beige_text else ""

prompt_5 = f"""You are a Federal Reserve staff economist writing the GDP & Output section of Tealbook A.
Style: declarative, third person. Every sentence names its exact time period and value.
Complete every sentence fully. Forward-looking: 'is projected to', 'appears on track to'.

IMPORTANT:
- {q_curr} = last RELEASED quarter (baseline only)
- {spf_q1} = CURRENT quarter underway (PRIMARY focus of this paragraph)
- {spf_q2} and beyond = near-term outlook
- Beige Book describes {spf_q1} conditions — this is your main forward-looking evidence.

BASELINE ({q_curr}, realized): GDP {_f(gdp_curr_v)}%
SPF PROJECTIONS (primary subject — not yet released):
{spf_lines}
PRIOR SPF VINTAGE (revision context):
{spf_prev_lines}
Potential GDP growth: {_f(pot_for_quarter(q_curr))}%
{bb_nearterm}

TASK — Write exactly 1 paragraph (4 sentences). Primary focus is {spf_q1}:
1. State what the SPF median projects for {spf_q1} GDP growth and characterize
   it as acceleration or deceleration from {q_curr}'s realized rate of {_f(gdp_curr_v)}%.
2. State whether the current SPF is an upward or downward revision from the prior
   vintage for {spf_q1}, citing both values explicitly.
3. Identify which component (PCE, nonres. fixed investment, or residential) is the
   primary driver of the {spf_q1} projection and state its projected rate.
4. If Beige Book is available, integrate one specific {spf_q1} observation that
   supports or complicates the projection; name {spf_q1} explicitly and use
   qualified language ('consistent with', 'may pose a risk to')."""

print(call_claude(prompt_5, max_tokens=350))
```

```{python}
#| label: gdp-narrative-6-medterm
#| output: asis

prompt_6 = f"""You are a Federal Reserve staff economist writing the GDP & Output section of Tealbook A.
Style: declarative, third person. Every sentence names its exact time period and value.
Complete every sentence fully. Forward-looking: 'is projected to', 'is expected to'.

Output gap ({og_date}): {og_latest:+.2f}% of potential GDP (CBO)
Potential GDP growth: {_f(pot_for_quarter(q_curr))}% (annualized)
FOMC longer-run real GDP: {fomc_lr_gdp}%
Latest actual GDP ({q_curr}): {_f(gdp_curr_v)}%
SPF forecasts: {' | '.join(f'{q}: {_f(spf_gdp.get(q))}%' for q in spf_qs)}

TASK — Write exactly 1 paragraph (3 complete sentences):
1. State the output gap as of {og_date} and characterize whether the economy
   is operating above or below potential and by how much.
2. Compare the SPF growth trajectory to both the CBO potential growth rate and the
   FOMC longer-run rate of {fomc_lr_gdp}%, noting the implied direction of the output gap.
3. Name one specific upside risk and one specific downside risk to convergence
   toward potential, each tied to a named GDP component — not generic statements."""

print(call_claude(prompt_6, max_tokens=260))
```

```{python}
#| label: gdp-narrative-7-context
#| output: asis

if not beige_text:
    print('*[Beige Book unavailable — contextual narrative skipped for this cycle.]*')
else:
    prompt_7 = f"""You are a Federal Reserve staff economist writing Tealbook A.
Style: declarative, third person. Qualified causal language: "consistent with",
"likely reflecting", "in part attributable to", "appeared to weigh on".
Do not invent facts. Do not use generic themes. Flowing prose — no bullet points.
Complete every sentence fully.

BEIGE BOOK NATIONAL SUMMARY ({beige_date}) — reflects {spf_q1} current conditions:
{beige_text}

GDP OUTCOMES ({q_curr}, most recent release):
Real GDP: {_f(gdp_curr_v)}% | PCE: {_fp(_cv('PCE'))} pp
Nonres. fixed inv.: {_fp(_cv('Nonres. fixed investment'))} pp
Residential inv.: {_fp(_cv('Residential investment'))} pp
Federal govt: {_fp(_cv('Federal government'))} pp
Inventories: {_fp(_cv('Change in inventories'))} pp | Net exports: {_fp(_cv('Net exports'))} pp
Output gap ({og_date}): {og_latest:+.2f}%

SPF projection for {spf_q1}: {_f(_sv('Real GDP', spf_q1))}%

TASK — Write exactly 3 sentences (4 if divergence requires explanation):
1. Identify the single most prominent theme in the Beige Book based on emphasis
   or repeated mentions; state which GDP component(s) from {q_curr} it most directly
   relates to, or explicitly note any divergence between the Beige Book ({spf_q1})
   and the GDP outcome ({q_curr}).
2. Explain the transmission channel using cautious language, referencing at least
   one specific numerical GDP contribution from {q_curr}.
3. Assess whether this theme is likely to affect the {spf_q1} projection of
   {_f(_sv('Real GDP', spf_q1))}%, using qualified language and naming {spf_q1}."""

    print('\n**Current Conditions Context**\n')
    print(call_claude(prompt_7, max_tokens=300))
```

```{python}
#| label: plot-gdp-1

gdp_yoy  = pull_series('GDPC1',           start=DATA_START).dropna().pct_change(4).mul(100).dropna()
gdi_raw  = pull_series('A261RX1Q020SBEA', start=DATA_START).dropna()
gdi_yoy  = gdi_raw.pct_change(4).mul(100).dropna()
gdi_date = quarter_label(pd.Timestamp(gdi_yoy.index[-1]))

fn = next_fig()
fig = go.Figure()
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=gdp_yoy[CHART_START_GDP:].index,
    y=gdp_yoy[CHART_START_GDP:].values, mode='lines',
    name='Real GDP', line=line(C1),
    hovertemplate='%{x|%Y Q%q}: %{y:.1f}%<extra></extra>'))
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=gdi_yoy[CHART_START_GDP:].index,
    y=gdi_yoy[CHART_START_GDP:].values, mode='lines',
    name=f'Real GDI (thru {gdi_date})', line=line(C2,'dash'),
    hovertemplate='%{x|%Y Q%q}: %{y:.1f}%<extra></extra>'))
fig.add_hline(y=0, line_color='black', line_width=0.8)
fig.update_layout(**base_layout(f'Figure {fn} — Real GDP & GDI', subtitle='4-quarter % change'))
fig.update_yaxes(ticksuffix='%')
show(fig, 'Source: BEA (GDPC1, A261RX1Q020SBEA) via FRED')
```

```{python}
#| label: plot-gdp-2

mfg_raw = pull_series('IPXXX001S', start=DATA_START).dropna()
mfg_3m  = (((mfg_raw / mfg_raw.shift(3)) ** 4) - 1).mul(100).dropna()[CHART_START_GDP:]

fn = next_fig()
fig = go.Figure()
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=mfg_3m.index, y=mfg_3m.values, mode='lines',
    name='Mfg IP ex. motor vehicles', line=line(C1),
    hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:.1f}%<extra></extra>'))
fig.add_hline(y=0, line_color='black', line_width=0.8)
fig.update_layout(**base_layout(f'Figure {fn} — Manufacturing IP ex. Motor Vehicles and Parts',
    subtitle='3-month percent change, annual rate'), showlegend=False)
fig.update_yaxes(ticksuffix='%')
show(fig, 'Source: FRB (IPXXX001S) via FRED', flush=True)
```

```{python}
#| label: plot-gdp-3

lv       = pull_series('ALTSALES',   start=DATA_START).dropna()[CHART_START_GDP:]
auto_asm = pull_series('MVAAUTLTTS', start=DATA_START).dropna()[CHART_START_GDP:]

fn = next_fig()
fig = go.Figure()
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=lv.index, y=lv.values, mode='lines',
    name='Sales', line=line(C1),
    hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:.2f}M<extra></extra>'))
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=auto_asm.index, y=auto_asm.values, mode='lines',
    name='Production (assembly)', line=line(C2),
    hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:.2f}M<extra></extra>'))
layout = base_layout(f'Figure {fn} — Sales and Production of Light Motor Vehicles',
                     subtitle='Millions of units, annual rate')
layout['yaxis']['autorange'] = True
fig.update_layout(**layout)
show(fig, "Source: BEA (ALTSALES) and FRB (MVAAUTLTTS) via FRED")
```

```{python}
#| label: plot-gdp-4

pce_m  = pull_series('PCEC96', start=DATA_START).dropna()
pce_6m = (((pce_m / pce_m.shift(6)) ** 2) - 1).mul(100).dropna()[CHART_START_GDP:]

fn = next_fig()
fig = go.Figure()
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=pce_6m.index, y=pce_6m.values, mode='lines',
    name='Real PCE', line=line(C4),
    hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:.1f}%<extra></extra>'))
fig.add_hline(y=0, line_color='black', line_width=0.8)
fig.update_layout(**base_layout(f'Figure {fn} — Real PCE Growth',
    subtitle='6-month percent change, annual rate'))
fig.update_yaxes(ticksuffix='%')
show(fig, 'Source: BEA (PCEC96) via FRED', flush=True)
```

```{python}
#| label: plot-gdp-5

sf_starts  = pull_series('HOUST1F', start=DATA_START).dropna()[CHART_START_GDP:]
sf_permits = pull_series('PERMIT1', start=DATA_START).dropna()[CHART_START_GDP:]

fn = next_fig()
fig = go.Figure()
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=sf_starts.index, y=sf_starts.values, mode='lines',
    name='Starts', line=line(C1),
    hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:.0f}K<extra></extra>'))
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=sf_permits.index, y=sf_permits.values, mode='lines',
    name='Permits', line=line(C2),
    hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:.0f}K<extra></extra>'))
fig.update_layout(**base_layout(f'Figure {fn} — Single-Family Housing Starts and Permits',
    subtitle='Thousands of units, annual rate'))
fig.update_yaxes(tickformat=',')
show(fig, 'Note: Tealbook uses adjusted permits.<br>Source: Census (HOUST1F, PERMIT1) via FRED')
```

```{python}
#| label: plot-gdp-6

exist  = pull_series('EXHOSLUSM495S', start='2003-01-01').dropna() / 1000
new_sf = pull_series('HSN1F',         start='2003-01-01').dropna() / 1000

fn = next_fig()
fig = go.Figure()
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=exist.index, y=exist.values, mode='lines',
    name='Existing (left)', yaxis='y1', line=line(C3),
    hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:.2f}M<extra></extra>'))
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=new_sf.index, y=new_sf.values, mode='lines',
    name='New SF (right)', yaxis='y2', line=line(C2),
    hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:.2f}M<extra></extra>'))
layout = base_layout(f'Figure {fn} — Home Sales',
    subtitle='Millions of units (annual rate)', start='2025-01-01', r_margin=55)
layout['yaxis']  = dict(showgrid=True, gridcolor='#eeeeee', zeroline=False,
                         tickfont=dict(size=9), autorange=True, tickformat='.2f')
layout['yaxis2'] = dict(overlaying='y', side='right', showgrid=False,
                         zeroline=False, tickfont=dict(size=9), autorange=True, tickformat='.2f')
layout['legend'] = dict(orientation='h', yanchor='top', y=-0.18,
                         xanchor='center', x=0.5, font=dict(size=9), bgcolor='rgba(0,0,0,0)')
layout['margin'] = dict(l=55, r=55, t=65, b=50)
fig.update_layout(**layout)
fig.update_xaxes(type='date', range=['2025-01-01', pd.Timestamp.now().strftime('%Y-%m-%d')],
                 dtick='M2', tickformat='%b %Y')
show(fig, 'Source: NAR (EXHOSLUSM495S) and Census (HSN1F) via FRED.', flush=True)
```

```{python}
#| label: plot-gdp-7

cap_o = (pull_series('NEWORDER', start=DATA_START).dropna().rolling(3).mean() / 1000)[CHART_START_GDP:]
cap_s = (pull_series('ANXAVS',   start=DATA_START).dropna().rolling(3).mean() / 1000)[CHART_START_GDP:]

fn = next_fig()
fig = go.Figure()
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=cap_o.index, y=cap_o.values, mode='lines',
    name='Orders', line=line(C2),
    hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: $%{y:.1f}B<extra></extra>'))
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=cap_s.index, y=cap_s.values, mode='lines',
    name='Shipments', line=line(C3),
    hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: $%{y:.1f}B<extra></extra>'))
fig.update_layout(**base_layout(f'Figure {fn} — Nondefense Cap. Goods ex. Aircraft',
    subtitle='Ratio scale, billions of dollars (3-mo MA)'))
fig.update_yaxes(type='log', tickformat='.0f')
show(fig, 'Note: Data are 3-month moving averages.<br>Source: Census (NEWORDER, ANXAVS) via FRED.')
```

```{python}
#| label: plot-gdp-8

nrc = (pull_series('TLNRESCONS', start=DATA_START).dropna() / 1000)[CHART_START_GDP:]

fn = next_fig()
fig = go.Figure()
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=nrc.index, y=nrc.values, mode='lines',
    name='Nonres. construction', line=line(C1),
    hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: $%{y:.1f}B<extra></extra>'))
layout = base_layout(f'Figure {fn} — Nonresidential Construction Put in Place',
    subtitle='Billions of dollars')
layout['yaxis']['autorange'] = True
fig.update_layout(**layout)
fig.update_yaxes(tickformat=',')
show(fig, 'Note: Nominal; Tealbook uses real BEA with staff deflator.<br>Source: Census (TLNRESCONS) via FRED.',
     flush=True)
```

```{python}
#| label: plot-gdp-9

inv_r = pull_series('ISRATIO', start=DATA_START).dropna()[CHART_START_GDP:]

fn = next_fig()
fig = go.Figure()
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=inv_r.index, y=inv_r.values, mode='lines',
    name='Inv/Sales ratio', line=line(C1),
    hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:.2f} mo<extra></extra>'))
layout = base_layout(f'Figure {fn} — Inventory Ratios', subtitle='Months')
layout['yaxis']['autorange'] = True
fig.update_layout(**layout)
show(fig, 'Note: Staff also calculates a flow-of-goods system.<br>Source: Census (ISRATIO) via FRED.')
```

```{python}
#| label: plot-gdp-10

exports = pull_series('EXPGSC1',         start=DATA_START).dropna()[CHART_START_GDP:]
imports = pull_series('A187RX1Q020SBEA', start=DATA_START).dropna()[CHART_START_GDP:]

fn = next_fig()
fig = go.Figure()
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=exports.index, y=exports.values, mode='lines',
    name='Exports', line=line(C1),
    hovertemplate='%{x|%Y Q%q}: $%{y:,.0f}B<extra></extra>'))
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=imports.index, y=imports.values, mode='lines',
    name='Non-oil imports', line=line(C2,'dash'),
    hovertemplate='%{x|%Y Q%q}: $%{y:,.0f}B<extra></extra>'))
layout = base_layout(f'Figure {fn} — Exports and Non-oil Imports',
    subtitle='Billions of chained 2017$, SAAR')
layout['yaxis']['autorange'] = True
fig.update_layout(**layout)
fig.update_yaxes(tickformat=',')
show(fig, 'Source: BEA (EXPGSC1, A187RX1Q020SBEA) via FRED.', flush=True)
```

---

## Labor Market

```{python}
#| label: labor-data
#| include: false

payrolls   = pull_series('PAYEMS',        start=DATA_START).dropna().diff().dropna()
unrate     = pull_series('UNRATE',        start=DATA_START).dropna()
nrou       = pull_series('NROU',          start=DATA_START).dropna().resample('MS').interpolate()
lfpr       = pull_series('CIVPART',       start=DATA_START).dropna()
epop25     = pull_series('LNS12300060',   start=DATA_START).dropna()
u5         = pull_series('U5RATE',        start=DATA_START).dropna()
ahe        = pull_series('CES0500000003', start=DATA_START).dropna().pct_change(12).mul(100).dropna()
jolts_o    = pull_series('JTSJOR',        start=DATA_START).dropna().rolling(3).mean()
jolts_q    = pull_series('JTSQUR',        start=DATA_START).dropna().rolling(3).mean()
jolts_h    = pull_series('JTS1000HIR',    start=DATA_START).dropna().rolling(3).mean()
claims     = pull_series('IC4WSA',        start=DATA_START).dropna()

unrate_latest  = unrate.iloc[-1]
unrate_date    = month_label(pd.Timestamp(unrate.index[-1]))
nrou_latest    = nrou.reindex(unrate.index, method='ffill').iloc[-1]
ugap           = unrate_latest - nrou_latest
payroll_3m     = payrolls.iloc[-3:].mean()
payroll_12m    = payrolls.iloc[-12:].mean()
payroll_date   = month_label(pd.Timestamp(payrolls.index[-1]))
lfpr_latest    = lfpr.iloc[-1]
lfpr_date      = month_label(pd.Timestamp(lfpr.index[-1]))
ahe_latest     = ahe.iloc[-1]
ahe_date       = month_label(pd.Timestamp(ahe.index[-1]))
claims_latest  = claims.iloc[-1]
claims_date    = month_label(pd.Timestamp(claims.index[-1]))
```

```{python}
#| label: labor-narrative
#| output: asis

prompt = f"""Labor market data:
- Unemployment rate ({unrate_date}): {unrate_latest:.1f}%; natural rate: {nrou_latest:.1f}%; gap: {ugap:+.1f} pp
- Nonfarm payrolls — 3-month avg through {payroll_date}: {payroll_3m:,.0f}K; 12-month avg: {payroll_12m:,.0f}K
- Labor force participation rate ({lfpr_date}): {lfpr_latest:.1f}%
- Average hourly earnings YoY ({ahe_date}): {ahe_latest:.1f}%
- Initial UI claims 4-week MA ({claims_date}): {claims_latest:,.0f}
- FOMC longer-run unemployment: {fomc_lr_unemp}%

Write 2 paragraphs. Paragraph 1: payroll growth, unemployment gap, labor market tightness.
Paragraph 2: wage growth, participation, and the outlook vs. FOMC longer-run.
Every sentence must name the specific month or period it references."""

print(call_claude(prompt))
```

```{python}
#| label: plot-labor-1

fn = next_fig()
fig = sp.make_subplots(rows=1, cols=3,
    subplot_titles=(f"Figure {fn} — Nonfarm Payrolls (thous.)",
                    f"Figure {fn+1} — Unemployment Rate (%)",
                    f"Figure {fn+2} — Avg Hourly Earnings YoY (%)"),
    horizontal_spacing=0.08)
_fig_count += 2

p = payrolls[CHART_START:]
fig.add_trace(go.Bar(x=p.index, y=p.values,
    marker_color=[C1 if v>=0 else C2 for v in p], name='Payrolls',
    hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:,.0f}K<extra></extra>'), row=1, col=1)
fig.add_hline(y=0, line_width=0.5, line_color='black', row=1, col=1)

ur = unrate[CHART_START:]
nr = nrou.reindex(ur.index, method='ffill')
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=ur.index, y=ur.values, name='U-3',
    line=line(C1), hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:.1f}%<extra></extra>'), row=1, col=2)
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=nr.index, y=nr.values, name='Natural rate',
    line=line(C2,'dash'), hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:.1f}%<extra></extra>'), row=1, col=2)
fig.add_hline(y=fomc_lr_unemp, line_width=0.9, line_color=C3, line_dash='dot',
    annotation_text=f'LR {fomc_lr_unemp}%', row=1, col=2)

a = ahe[CHART_START:]
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=a.index, y=a.values, name='AHE YoY',
    line=line(C1), hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:.1f}%<extra></extra>'), row=1, col=3)
fig.add_hline(y=0, line_width=0.5, line_color='black', row=1, col=3)

fig.update_layout(**fed_layout(height=420))
fig.update_xaxes(range=[CHART_START, TODAY.strftime('%Y-%m-%d')])
fig.show()
```

```{python}
#| label: plot-labor-2

fn = next_fig()
fig = sp.make_subplots(rows=1, cols=3,
    subplot_titles=(f"Figure {fn} — JOLTS Rates (%, 3-mo MA)",
                    f"Figure {fn+1} — Labor Force Participation & E-Pop (%)",
                    f"Figure {fn+2} — Initial UI Claims (thous., 4-wk MA)"),
    horizontal_spacing=0.08)
_fig_count += 2

for s, name, color, dash in [
    (jolts_o, 'Openings', C1, 'solid'),
    (jolts_q, 'Quits',    C2, 'dash'),
    (jolts_h, 'Hires',    C3, 'dot'),
]:
    s2 = s[CHART_START:].dropna()
    fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=s2.index, y=s2.values, name=name,
        line=line(color, dash), hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:.1f}%<extra></extra>'), row=1, col=1)

lf = lfpr[CHART_START:]
ep = epop25[CHART_START:]
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=lf.index, y=lf.values, name='LFPR (total)',
    line=line(C1), hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:.1f}%<extra></extra>'), row=1, col=2)
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=ep.index, y=ep.values, name='E-Pop 25-54',
    line=line(C2,'dash'), hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:.1f}%<extra></extra>'), row=1, col=2)

c = claims[CHART_START:]
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=c.index, y=c.values, name='Initial claims',
    line=line(C5), hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:,.0f}K<extra></extra>'), row=1, col=3)

fig.update_layout(**fed_layout(height=420))
fig.update_xaxes(range=[CHART_START, TODAY.strftime('%Y-%m-%d')])
fig.show()
```

---

## Inflation

```{python}
#| label: inflation-data
#| include: false

cpi      = pull_series('CPIAUCSL',             start=DATA_START).dropna().pct_change(12).mul(100).dropna()
pce      = pull_series('PCEPI',                start=DATA_START).dropna().pct_change(12).mul(100).dropna()
core_pce = pull_series('PCEPILFE',             start=DATA_START).dropna().pct_change(12).mul(100).dropna()
trim_pce = pull_series('PCETRIM12M159SFRBDAL', start=DATA_START).dropna()
mkt_pce  = pull_series('DPCXRG3M086SBEA',      start=DATA_START).dropna().pct_change(12).mul(100).dropna()
eci      = pull_series('ECIWAG',               start=DATA_START).dropna().pct_change(4).mul(100).dropna()
ahe_inf  = pull_series('CES0500000003',        start=DATA_START).dropna().pct_change(12).mul(100).dropna()
tips_fwd = pull_series('T5YIFRM',              start=DATA_START).dropna()
clev5    = pull_series('EXPINF5YR',            start=DATA_START).dropna()

cpi_latest   = cpi.iloc[-1];     cpi_date_inf  = month_label(pd.Timestamp(cpi.index[-1]))
pce_latest   = pce.iloc[-1];     pce_date_inf  = month_label(pd.Timestamp(pce.index[-1]))
core_latest  = core_pce.iloc[-1]; core_date_inf = month_label(pd.Timestamp(core_pce.index[-1]))
trim_latest  = trim_pce.iloc[-1]; trim_date     = month_label(pd.Timestamp(trim_pce.index[-1]))
mkt_latest   = mkt_pce.iloc[-1]; mkt_date      = month_label(pd.Timestamp(mkt_pce.index[-1]))
eci_latest   = eci.iloc[-1];     eci_date      = quarter_label(pd.Timestamp(eci.index[-1]))
tips_latest  = tips_fwd.iloc[-1]; tips_date     = month_label(pd.Timestamp(tips_fwd.index[-1]))
```

```{python}
#| label: inflation-narrative
#| output: asis

prompt = f"""Inflation data:
- Headline PCE YoY ({pce_date_inf}): {pce_latest:.1f}%
- Core PCE YoY ({core_date_inf}): {core_latest:.1f}%; Fed target: {fomc_lr_pce:.1f}%
- Trimmed mean PCE 12-month ({trim_date}): {trim_latest:.1f}%
- Market-based core PCE YoY ({mkt_date}): {mkt_latest:.1f}%
- Headline CPI YoY ({cpi_date_inf}): {cpi_latest:.1f}%
- ECI private wages YoY ({eci_date}): {eci_latest:.1f}%
- 5yr5yr TIPS forward ({tips_date}): {tips_latest:.2f}%
- FOMC longer-run PCE target: {fomc_lr_pce:.1f}%
- SPF PCE next year: {spf_pce_next}%

Write 2 paragraphs. Paragraph 1: recent price developments across measures.
Paragraph 2: labor cost pressures and inflation expectations vs. target.
Every sentence must name the specific period it references."""

print(call_claude(prompt))
```

```{python}
#| label: plot-inflation

fn = next_fig()
fig = sp.make_subplots(rows=1, cols=3,
    subplot_titles=(f"Figure {fn} — Headline CPI & PCE (YoY %)",
                    f"Figure {fn+1} — Core PCE Measures (YoY %)",
                    f"Figure {fn+2} — Inflation Expectations (%)"),
    horizontal_spacing=0.08)
_fig_count += 2

for s, name, color, dash in [(cpi,'CPI',C2,'solid'),(pce,'PCE',C3,'dash')]:
    s2 = s[CHART_START:]
    fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=s2.index, y=s2.values, name=name,
        line=line(color,dash), hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:.2f}%<extra></extra>'), row=1, col=1)
fig.add_hline(y=2.0, line_width=0.9, line_color='black', line_dash='dot',
    annotation_text='2%', row=1, col=1)

for s, name, color, dash in [
    (trim_pce,'Trimmed mean',C1,'solid'),
    (mkt_pce, 'Market-based',C2,'solid'),
    (core_pce,'Core PCE',    C3,'dot'),
]:
    s2 = s[CHART_START:]
    fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=s2.index, y=s2.values, name=name,
        line=line(color,dash), hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:.2f}%<extra></extra>'), row=1, col=2)
fig.add_hline(y=2.0, line_width=0.9, line_color='black', line_dash='dot', row=1, col=2)

for s, name, color, dash in [
    (tips_fwd,'5yr5yr TIPS',C2,'solid'),
    (clev5,   'Cleveland 5-yr',C1,'dash'),
]:
    s2 = s[CHART_START:]
    fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=s2.index, y=s2.values, name=name,
        line=line(color,dash), hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:.2f}%<extra></extra>'), row=1, col=3)
fig.add_hline(y=2.0, line_width=0.9, line_color='black', line_dash='dot',
    annotation_text='2%', row=1, col=3)

fig.update_layout(**fed_layout(height=420))
fig.update_xaxes(range=[CHART_START, TODAY.strftime('%Y-%m-%d')])
fig.show()
```

---

## Financial Conditions

```{python}
#| label: fin-data
#| include: false

dgs10  = pull_series('DGS10',        start=DATA_START).dropna().resample('MS').mean()
dgs2   = pull_series('DGS2',         start=DATA_START).dropna().resample('MS').mean()
dgs3m  = pull_series('DGS3MO',       start=DATA_START).dropna().resample('MS').mean()
hy_spr = pull_series('BAMLH0A0HYM2', start=DATA_START).dropna().resample('MS').mean()
ig_spr = pull_series('BAMLC0A4CBBB', start=DATA_START).dropna().resample('MS').mean()
vix    = pull_series('VIXCLS',        start=DATA_START).dropna().resample('MS').mean()
nfci   = pull_series('NFCI',          start=DATA_START).dropna()

spread_series = (dgs10 - dgs2).dropna()
spread_10_2   = spread_series.iloc[-1]
t10_date      = month_label(pd.Timestamp(dgs10.index[-1]))
spr_date      = month_label(pd.Timestamp(hy_spr.index[-1]))
vix_date      = month_label(pd.Timestamp(vix.index[-1]))
```

```{python}
#| label: plot-financial

fn = next_fig()
fig = sp.make_subplots(rows=1, cols=3,
    subplot_titles=(f"Figure {fn} — Treasury Yields & Spread (%)",
                    f"Figure {fn+1} — Credit Spreads (bp, OAS)",
                    f"Figure {fn+2} — VIX"),
    horizontal_spacing=0.08)
_fig_count += 2

for s, name, color, dash in [
    (dgs10,         '10-yr',        C1, 'solid'),
    (dgs2,          '2-yr',         C2, 'dash'),
    (spread_series, '10s-2s spread',C3, 'dot'),
]:
    s2 = s[CHART_START:]
    fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=s2.index, y=s2.values, name=name,
        line=line(color,dash), hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:.2f}%<extra></extra>'), row=1, col=1)
fig.add_hline(y=0, line_width=0.5, line_color='black', row=1, col=1)

for s, name, color in [(hy_spr,'HY (OAS)',C2),(ig_spr,'BBB (OAS)',C1)]:
    s2 = s[CHART_START:]
    fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=s2.index, y=s2.values, name=name,
        line=line(color), hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:.0f} bp<extra></extra>'), row=1, col=2)

v = vix[CHART_START:]
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=v.index, y=v.values, name='VIX',
    line=line(C5), hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:.1f}<extra></extra>'), row=1, col=3)

fig.update_layout(**fed_layout(height=420))
fig.update_xaxes(range=[CHART_START, TODAY.strftime('%Y-%m-%d')])
fig.show()
```

---

## International

---

## Special Topics

```{python}
#| label: special-data
#| include: false

def fetch_fed_speeches(n=6):
    url = "https://www.federalreserve.gov/feeds/speeches.xml"
    try:
        with urllib.request.urlopen(url, timeout=10) as r:
            content = r.read().decode('utf-8', errors='replace')
        items = re.findall(r'<item>(.*?)</item>', content, re.DOTALL)[:n]
        out = []
        for item in items:
            t = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', item)
            d = re.search(r'<dc:date>(.*?)</dc:date>', item)
            l = re.search(r'<link>(.*?)</link>', item)
            s = re.search(r'<dc:creator>(.*?)</dc:creator>', item)
            out.append({'title': t.group(1).strip() if t else 'Untitled',
                        'date':  d.group(1)[:10]    if d else '',
                        'url':   l.group(1).strip() if l else '#',
                        'speaker': s.group(1).strip() if s else 'Federal Reserve'})
        return out
    except Exception:
        return []

def fetch_feds_papers(n=4):
    url = "https://www.federalreserve.gov/feeds/feds.xml"
    try:
        with urllib.request.urlopen(url, timeout=10) as r:
            content = r.read().decode('utf-8', errors='replace')
        items = re.findall(r'<item>(.*?)</item>', content, re.DOTALL)[:n]
        out = []
        for item in items:
            t  = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', item)
            d  = re.search(r'<dc:date>(.*?)</dc:date>', item)
            l  = re.search(r'<link>(.*?)</link>', item)
            dc = re.search(r'<description>(.*?)</description>', item, re.DOTALL)
            out.append({'title': t.group(1).strip() if t else 'Untitled',
                        'date':  d.group(1)[:10]    if d else '',
                        'url':   l.group(1).strip() if l else '#',
                        'desc':  (re.sub(r'<.*?>','',dc.group(1)).strip()[:200]+'...') if dc else ''})
        return out
    except Exception:
        return []

speeches = fetch_fed_speeches()
papers   = fetch_feds_papers()
```

```{python}
#| label: special-narrative
#| output: asis

speech_lines = "\n".join(
    f"- [{s['date']}] {s['speaker']}: \"{s['title']}\""
    for s in speeches) or "- No speeches retrieved"

paper_lines = "\n".join(
    f"- [{p['date']}] \"{p['title']}\""
    for p in papers) or "- No papers retrieved"

prompt = f"""Today: {now_str}. Upcoming FOMC cycle special topic identification.

Recent Federal Reserve speeches:
{speech_lines}

Recent FEDS working papers:
{paper_lines}

Current conditions: Core PCE {core_latest:.1f}% ({core_date}),
unemployment gap {ugap:+.1f} pp ({unrate_date}),
10s-2s spread {spread_10_2:+.2f} pp ({t10_date}).

Propose 3 potential Tealbook special topics for the next FOMC meeting.
For each topic: 1) concise title (under 10 words), 2) one sentence on policy
relevance citing specific data, 3) one sentence on the anchoring analysis.
Number them clearly."""

print("### Suggested Special Topics\n")
print(call_claude(prompt, max_tokens=550))
```

```{python}
#| label: special-tables
from IPython.display import display, HTML

if speeches:
    rows = ""
    for i, s in enumerate(speeches):
        bg = "#f5f7fb" if i % 2 == 0 else "white"
        rows += f"""<tr style="background:{bg};">
          <td style="padding:6px 10px;color:#555;">{s['date']}</td>
          <td style="padding:6px 10px;">{s['speaker']}</td>
          <td style="padding:6px 10px;">
            <a href="{s['url']}" target="_blank" style="color:{C1};">{s['title']}</a>
          </td></tr>"""
    display(HTML(f"""
    <h4 style="color:{C1};">Recent Federal Reserve Speeches</h4>
    <table style="width:100%;border-collapse:collapse;font-size:0.88rem;">
      <thead><tr style="background:{C1};color:white;">
        <th style="padding:7px 10px;">Date</th>
        <th style="padding:7px 10px;">Speaker</th>
        <th style="padding:7px 10px;">Title</th>
      </tr></thead><tbody>{rows}</tbody></table>"""))

if papers:
    rows = ""
    for i, p in enumerate(papers):
        bg = "#f5f7fb" if i % 2 == 0 else "white"
        rows += f"""<tr style="background:{bg};">
          <td style="padding:6px 10px;color:#555;">{p['date']}</td>
          <td style="padding:6px 10px;">
            <a href="{p['url']}" target="_blank" style="color:{C1};">{p['title']}</a>
            <br><span style="font-size:0.80rem;color:#666;">{p['desc']}</span>
          </td></tr>"""
    display(HTML(f"""
    <h4 style="color:{C1};margin-top:1.4rem;">Recent FEDS Working Papers</h4>
    <table style="width:100%;border-collapse:collapse;font-size:0.88rem;">
      <thead><tr style="background:{C1};color:white;">
        <th style="padding:7px 10px;">Date</th>
        <th style="padding:7px 10px;">Title & Abstract</th>
      </tr></thead><tbody>{rows}</tbody></table>"""))
```

:::
